q: If \[U=\left \{ -5,-4,-3,-2,0,1,2,3,5 \right \}, C=\left \{ -5,-4,-2,0,2,5 \right \},\] and \[D=\left \{ -5,0,1,2,3 \right \},\] then which of the following resultant sets verifies De Morgan’s Law?
skill: State and verify De Morgan’s law (ii)
feedback:
De Morgan’s Law is stated as:
\[(C∩D)'=C'∪D'\]

Find the left-hand side of the expression:
\[(C∩D)'\]

\[(C∩D)'=U-(C∩D)\]
\[=\left \{ -5,-4,-3,-2,0,1,2,3,5 \right \}-(\left \{ -5,-4,-2,0,2,5 \right \}∩\left \{ -5,0,1,2,3 \right \})\]
\[=\left \{ -5,-4,-3,-2,0,1,2,3,5 \right \}-\left \{ -5,0,2 \right \}\]
\[=\left \{ -4,-3,-2,1,3,5 \right \}\]

Since the expression on the left-hand side of De Morgan’s Law is always equal to the expression on the right-hand side, the resultant set that verifies De Morgan’s law is \[\left \{ -4,-3,-2,1,3,5 \right \}.\]

subject: Mathematics
q: If \[U=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,4,5 \right \}, A=\left \{ 2,4,3 \right \},\] and \[B=\left \{ 0,1,4 \right \},\] then which of the following resultant sets verifies \[(A∩B)'=A'∪B' ?\]
skill: State and verify De Morgan’s law (ii)
feedback:
De Morgan’s Law is stated as:
\[(A∩B)'=A'∪B'\]

Find the left-hand side of the expression:
\[(A∩B)'\]

\[(A∩B)'=U-(A∩B)\]
\[=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,4,5 \right \}-(\left \{ 2,4,3 \right \}∩\left \{ 0,1,4 \right \})\]
\[=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,4,5 \right \}-\left \{ 4 \right \}\]
\[=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,5 \right \}\]

Now, find the right-hand side of the expression:
\[A'∪B'\]

\[A'∪ B'=(U-A)∪(U-B)\]
\[=(\left \{ 0,1,2,3,4,5 \right \}-\left \{ 2,4,3 \right \})∪(\left \{ 0,1,2,3,4,5 \right \}-\left \{ 0,1,4 \right \})\]
\[=\left \{ 0,1,5 \right \}∪\left \{ 2,3,5 \right \}\]
\[=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,5 \right \}\]

Since,
\[(A∩B)'=A'∪B'=\left \{ 0,1,2,3,5 \right \},\] we have proved the De Morgan’s law.

q: The law which shows the relationship between union, intersection and compliment is called the _____.
skill: State and verify De Morgan’s law (ii)
feedback:
De Morgan’s Law relates the three operations to each other. These operations are union, intersection and complement.

Associative, distributive and commutative laws also relate union and intersection but they do not deal with complement.

q: The next two terms in the sequence \[ 4374, -1458, 486, -162, 54, ...\] will be _____.
skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences
feedback:
\[4,374\] divided by \[-3\] becomes \[-1,458\]. Furthermore, \[-1,458\] when divided by \[-3\] results in \[486\].
The same pattern is followed throughout this sequence.



We divide \[54\] by \[-3\] to get the next term and the resulting term will be further divided by -3 as well.

\begin{align} 54 \div -3& = -18\\ -18 \div -3& = 6\\ \end{align}

The next two terms in the sequence \[ 4374, -1458, 486, -162, 54,...\] are \[-18\] and \[6\].

feedback:

To find the value of \[a\], we multiply \[18\] with \[3\]. The value of \[a\] is 54.

q: In the sequence \[55,60,65,70,75\], the common difference is _____.
skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences
feedback:
\begin{align} \mbox{Difference between first } 2 \mbox{ terms } & = \mbox{Term }2 - \mbox{Term }1\\ & = 60 - 55\\ & = 5\\ \mbox{Difference between second } 2 \mbox{ terms } & = \mbox{Term }3 - \mbox{Term }2\\ & = 65 - 60\\ & = 5 \end{align}



In the sequence \[55,60,65,70,75\] the difference between any two consecutive terms is +5, thus the common difference in this sequence is \[+5\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In the sequence \[37,30,23,16, ...\], the smallest positive integer will be _____.
skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences
feedback:
In order to find the smallest positive number of this sequence, we must find the next terms of the sequence.
For that we must calculate the common difference of this sequence.

\begin{align} \mbox{Term }2 - \mbox{Term }1& = 30 - 37\\ & = -7\\ \mbox{Term }3 - \mbox{Term }2& = 23 - 30\\ & = -7\\ \end{align}
As the difference between two consecutive terms of this sequence is \[-7\], we know that:
Common Difference \[= -7\]

We get the next terms by adding the common difference to the previous terms.
The next three terms are \[9, 2\] and \[-5\] and the sequence becomes \[37, 30, 23, 16, 9, 2, -5\]...



Thus, the smallest positive number in this sequence is \[2\].

skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences

subject: Mathematics
q: The next two terms of the sequence \[-13,-30,-47,-64,...\] are _____.
skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences
feedback:
\begin{align} \mbox{Difference between first } 2 \mbox{ terms } & = \mbox{Term }2 - \mbox{Term }1\\ & = -30 - (-13)\\ & = -30 + 13\\ & = -17\\ \mbox{Difference between second } 2 \mbox{ terms } & = \mbox{Term }3 - \mbox{Term }2\\ & = -47 - (-30)\\ & = -47 + 30\\ & = -17\\ \end{align}

In the sequence \[3,9,15,21,27,33\] the difference between two consecutive terms is -17 which means that the common difference in this sequence is -17.

\begin{align} \mbox{Term }5 & = \mbox{Term }4 + \mbox{Common difference}\\ & = -64 + (-17)\\ & = -81\\ \mbox{Term }6 & = \mbox{Term }5 + \mbox{Common difference}\\ & = -81 + -17\\ & = -98\\ \end{align}

Thus, the next two terms in the sequence are \[-81\] and \[-98\].

subject: Mathematics
q: The common difference between the terms of a sequence is \[+18\]. If the third term of the sequence is \[126\], what will the first and the second term of the sequence be?
skill: Recognise simple patterns from various number sequences
feedback:
\begin{align} \mbox{Common Difference } & = +18\\ \mbox{Term }3& = 126\\ \mbox{Term }3& = \mbox{Term }2 + \mbox{ Common Difference}\\ 126& = \mbox{Term }2 + 18\\ \mbox{Solving for Term 2:}\\ 126 - 18& = \mbox{Term }2\\ 108& = \mbox{Term }2\\ \mbox{Term }2& = 108\\\\ \mbox{Similarly, Term }1& = \mbox{Term }2 - 18\\ &= 108 - 18\\ \mbox{Term }1& = 90 \end{align}

The first two terms in the sequence are \[90\] and \[108\].

The sequence \[2000,2004,2008,2012,2016\] is the sequence representing leap years.

The sequence \[2000,2005,2010,2015,2020\] has a common difference of \[+5\].
\[2000,2006,2012,2018,2024\] has a common difference of \[+6\].

q:
\[A.P = 6, 11, 16, 21, 26…\]
Which of the following is an expression, in terms of \[n\] , for the \[n\]th term of the sequence?
skill: Find the nth or the general term of an arithmetic sequence
feedback:
Given terms are \[6, 11, 16, 21, 26…\]

\[a = 6\]
\[d = 11 – 6 = 5\]

The general or \[n\]th term of an \[A.P\] is \[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

Putting values of \[a\] and \[d\]

\[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

\[= 6 + (n – 1)5\]

\[= 6 + 5_n – 5\]

\[= 5_n + 1\]

\[n = 40\]

q: How many terms are there in an arithmetic progression, where \[a_1 = 4, d = 3, a_n = 73?\]
skill: Find the nth or the general term of an arithmetic sequence
feedback:
From the given information;
\[d = 3\]
\[a_n = 73\]
\[a = 4\]
\[n = ?\]

Putting values into formula for general term,
\[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

\[73 = 4 + (n – 1)3\]

\[73 = 4 + 3_n – 3\]

\[73 = 1 + 3_n\]

\[73 – 1 = 3_n\]

\[3_n = 72\]

\[n = \frac{72}{3}=24\]

There are \[24\] terms in the \[AP\]

q: If the \[5^{th}\] term of an arithmetic progression \[(A.P)\] is \[14\] and \[20^{th}\] term is \[50\] , what is the \[15^{th}\] term?
skill: Find the nth or the general term of an arithmetic sequence
feedback:
We have the values;

\[5^{th}\] term is \[14\] , or \[a_5 = 14\] and \[n = 5\]

Putting these values in the formula for general term:

\[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

\[a + (5 – 1)d = 14\]

\[a + 4d = 14\]

\[a = 14\, – 4d\]

\[20^{th}\] term is \[59\] , or \[a_{20} = 59\]

Putting this in the formula for general term \[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

\[a + (20\, – 1)d = 59\]

\[a + 19d= 59\]

\[a = 59 \, – 19d\]

Therefore,

\[14 \, – 4d = 59\, – 19d\]

\[– 4d + 19d = 59\, – 14\]

\[15d = 45\]

\[d = 3\]

Thus, \[a = 14 \, – 4(3) = 14\, – 12 = 2\]

Solving for \[a_{15}\] where \[n = 15\] ,

\[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

\[a_{15} = 2 + (15\, – 1)3 = 2 + (14)3 = 2 + 42 = 44\]

\[a = a_1 = 7\]

\[d = 13\, – 7 = 6\]

\[a_{11} = ?\]

\[n = 11\]

We know that \[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

Putting values of \[a, d\] and \[n\] in the formula

\[a_{11} = 6 + (11-1)6\]

\[= 6+ (10)6\]

\[= 66\]

\[a_{11}= 66\]

q: Which term of an arithmetic progression \[(A.P)\: 6, 2, -2…\] is \[-146\] ?
skill: Find the nth or the general term of an arithmetic sequence
feedback:
Given terms are \[6, 2, -2\]
\[a = 6\]
\[d = 2\, – 6 = - 4\]
\[a_n = 146\]
\[n =?\]

The general or \[n\]th term of an \[A.P\] is \[a_n = a + (n – 1)d\]

Putting values of \[a, a_n\] and \[d\]

\[-146 = 6 + (n – 1) (-4)\]

\[-146 = 6\, – 4_n +4\]

\[4_n = 6 + 4 + 146 = 156\]

\[n = \frac{156}{4}=39\]

q:
The following pattern contains diamonds and triangles. What term number will contain \[356\] triangles?



figure number (\[n\]) number of diamonds (\[d\]) number of triangles (\[t\])
\[1\] \[3\] \[12\]
\[2\] \[5\] \[20\]
\[3\] \[7\] \[28\]
? ? ?
\[n\]
skill: Solve problems involving number sequences and number patterns
feedback:
To find the term containing \[356\] triangles, first we need to find the general term for the number of triangles. Then, we will place the value of \[t\] into this term. We can see that number of triangles in the first term is \[12\], \[20\] in the second and \[28\] in the third. Therefore, the common difference between the values is \[8\]. We will multiply the common difference with the position number \[n\], giving us \[8n\]. The aim is to create a general equation related to the figure number. The value is still \[4\] less than the number of triangles, so we can add \[4\] to the general equation. This gives us \[8n + 4\], which is the general term for the number of triangles. Next, we will put the value of \[t\] into this term.

\begin{align} \mbox{t}&= 8\mbox{n} +4\\ 356&= 8\mbox{n} +4\\ 356 - 4&= 8\mbox{n}\\ \frac{352}{8}&=n\\\\ n&=44\\ \end{align}

The \[44\]th term contains \[356\] triangles.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The following dots pattern follows a number sequence shown in the table. Which term will contain \[250\] dots?



figure number (\[n\]) number of dots (\[d\])
\[1\] \[4\]
\[2\] \[10\]
\[3\] \[16\]
\[4\] \[22\]
? ?
\[n\]
skill: Solve problems involving number sequences and number patterns
feedback:
To find the term containing \[250\] dots, first we need to find the general term of the number of dots. Then, we will place the value of \[d\] into this term. We can see that number of dots in the first term is \[4\], \[10\] in the second and \[16\] in the third. Therefore, the common difference between the values is \[6\]. We will multiply the common difference with the position number \[n\], giving us \[6n\]. The aim is to create a general equation related to the figure number. The value is still \[2\] more than the number of dots, so we can subtract \[2\] from the general equation. This gives us \[6n - 2\], which is the general term for the number of dots. Next, we will put the value of \[d\] into this term.

\begin{align} \mbox{d}&= 6\mbox{n} -2\\ 250&= 6\mbox{n} -2\\ 250 + 2&= 6\mbox{n}\\ \frac{252}{6}&=n\\\\ n&=42\\ \end{align}

The \[42\]nd term contains \[250\] dots.

We can see that number of parallelograms in the first term is \[2\], \[6\] in the second and \[10\] in the third. Therefore, the common difference between the values is \[4\]. q:
This pattern follows a number sequence shown in the table. Which term will have \[45\] black squares?

\[n\]

'
skill: Solve problems involving number sequences and number patterns
feedback:
To find out which term has \[45\] black squares, first we need to find the general term for the number of black squares. We can see in the table that the number of black squares starts with \[3\] and increases to \[5\] and then to \[7\] with each successive term. So, the common difference between them is \[2\]. We will multiply the common difference with the position number \[n\], which gives us \[2n\]. The value is still \[1\] less than the number of black squares, so we can add \[1\] to the general equation. This gives us \[2n + 1\], which is the general term for the number of black squares. Next, we will put the value of \[b\] in this equation.

\begin{align} \mbox{b}&= 2\mbox{n} + 1\\ 45&= 2\mbox{n} + 1\\ 45 - 1&= 2\mbox{n}\\ \frac{44}{2}&=n\\\\ n&=22\\ \end{align}

The \[22\]nd term contains \[45\] black squares.

subject: Mathematics
q: \[\frac{1}{8}x^3+\frac{3}{4}x^2-9 \] is an algebraic _____.
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback: An expression in which variables and constants are connected by arithmetic operations of addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division is called an algebraic expression. Therefore, \[\frac{1}{8}x^3+\frac{3}{4}x^2-9\] is an algebraic expression.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Identify the literals in the following algebraic expression.

\[axy+bx+cy+d\]
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
The letters that are used to represent constants or coefficients are called literals.

Therefore, in the given expression, \[a,b,c,\] and \[d\] are literals, and \[x\] and \[y\] are variables.

subject: Mathematics
q:
What is the coefficient of \[x^4\] in the algebraic expression given below?

\[x^4-2x^2+3x\]
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
A coefficient is a constant by which a variable is multiplied. Therefore, the coefficient of \[x^4\] is \[1\], whereas the coefficients of \[x^2\] and \[x\] are \[-2\] and \[3\], respectively.

Note that the coefficient of \[x^4\] cannot be \[0\] because any term multiplied by \[0\] is \[0\], that is \[x \times 0=0\].

subject: Mathematics
q:
How many terms are there in the given algebraic expression?

\[y^3z-\frac{5z}{y^2}+6y \times 3z^2-4z+8\]
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
Each part of an algebraic expression that is separated by a plus or minus sign is called the term of an algebraic expression. Therefore, the number of terms in the given expression is \[5\].

Note that a multiplication or a division sign does not separate terms.

subject: Mathematics
q:
In the given algebraic expression, \[-12\] is a _____.

\[1-64a^3-12a+48a^2\]
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
A symbol that has a fixed numerical value is called a constant.

A variable is a symbol that has no fixed value but can take various numerical values.

The letters that are used to represent constants or coefficients are called literals.

A coefficient is a constant by which a variable is multiplied.

So, in the given expression, \[-12\], \[48\], and \[-64\] are coefficients, \[1\] is a constant, and \[a\] is a variable.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following algebraic expressions has \[4\] terms and \[2\] variables?
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
Each part of an algebraic expression that is separated by a plus or minus sign is called the term of an algebraic expression.

A variable is a symbol that has no fixed value but can take various numerical values.

The algebraic expression \[8x^3y^2+3xy^2+y^2+x\] has four terms and \[2\] variables \[x\] and \[y\].

Note that in the expression \[ax^2+byz-cz^3+9\], \[a\], \[b\], and \[c\] are literals, whereas \[x\], \[y\], and \[z\] are variables.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following expressions has \[9\] as a coefficient and \[5\] as a constant?
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
A symbol that has a fixed numerical value is called a constant.

A coefficient is a constant by which a variable is multiplied.

Therefore, in the expression \[9x+5\], \[9\] is the coefficient and \[5\] is the constant.

subject: Mathematics
q: Select an algebraic expression that is NOT in two variables.
skill: Recall constant, variable, literal and algebraic expression
feedback:
A variable is a symbol that has no fixed value but can take various numerical values.

\[18x^3y+5yz^2+ 12xz+5\] is an expression in \[3\] variables that are \[x\], \[y\], and \[z\], whereas the remaining three expressions are in \[2\] variables that are \[x\] and \[y\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If the expression \[\triangle + 5 = 8\] is written as \[2 + 5 = 8\], it is called a/an _____ statement.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback: If the expression \[\triangle + 5 = 8\] is written as \[2 + 5 = 8\], it is called a false statement. The reason for this is that the left-hand side of the equation is not equal to the right-hand side.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the expression \[\triangle + 3 = 4\] is written as \[1 + 3 = 4\], it is called a/an _____ statement.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback:
The expression \[1 + 3 = 4\] is called a true statement as the left-hand side is equal to the right-hand side.
Note that the expression, \[\triangle + 3 = 4\] is called an open statement.

subject: Mathematics
q: An algebraic sentence that can either be true or false is known as a/an _____.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback:
A sentence that can either be true or false is known as an open statement.

A true statement is called an equation:
\[x + 4 = 7\]

Using this equation, if we substitute \[x = 3\], the equation becomes \[3 + 4 = 7\], which is a true statement as left-hand side of the equation is equal to the right-hand side.

However, if we substitute \[x = 4\], the equation becomes \[4 + 4 = 7\]. In this, the left-hand side of the equation is equal to \[8\] while right-hand side is \[8\]. So, this is not a true statement.

subject: Mathematics
q: Mathematically, a true number statement is also called a/an _____.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback:
A true number statement is also called an equation.

Since the left-hand side of the equal sign is equal to the right-hand side, so it is called an equation.

\[5 + 4 = 9\]

\[9 = 9\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In order for an algebraic statement to be false, the left-hand side of the equation would  _____  the right-hand side.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback:
For an algebraic statement to be false, the left-hand side of the equation must not equal to the right-hand side. \[8+3 \neq 12\]

\[11 \neq 12\]

subject: Mathematics
q: For an algebraic statement to be true, the left-hand side of the equation must be _____ the right-hand side.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback:
For an algebraic statement to be true, the left-hand side of the equation must be equal to the right-hand side.

For e.g. \[2 + 3 = 5\]

5 = 5

subject: Mathematics
q: The symbol _____ stands for the unknown number in an expression.
skill: Algebra as an extension of arithmetic
feedback: The \[\triangle\] known as delta stands for the unknown number in an expression.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Write the algebraic expression for:
Three times \[a\] added to four times \[b\].
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
Three times \[a = 3a\]

Four times \[b = 4b\]

So, three times \[a\] added to four times \[b\] \[= 3a + 4b\]

subject: Mathematics
q: If the length of a rectangle is \[3y\,\ \mbox{cm}\] and its width is \[n \,\ \mbox{cm}\], what is the area of the rectangle?
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
Area of rectangle \[=\mbox{length}\times\mbox{width}\]

Area \[= 3y \times n\]

Area \[= 3yn \,\ \mbox{cm}^2\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Which of following does NOT correctly represent an algebraic expression for:

Twice the variable \[p\] divided by the product of \[6\] and \[m\].
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
\[p +p\div6\times m\] is not an algebraic expression. Let's see why:
\[= p + p\div6\times m\]

According to DMAS, we first division:

\[= p+\frac{p}{6}\times m\]

Next, we multiply:

\[= p +\frac{pm}{6}\]

Then, calculating the L.C.M to obtain the final expression:

\[=\frac{6p + pm}{6}\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Write an algebraic expression for the following:

Add \[5y\] to the product of \[a\] and \[b\].
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
The product of \[a\] and \[b\] \[= a \times b = ab\]

Adding \[5y\] to the product of \[a\] and \[b\] will result in:

\[5y + ab\]

subject: Mathematics
q: If Sara is \[x\] years old now, how old will she be after \[20\] years?
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
To calculate Sara's age after \[20\] years, we add her current age to \[20\] giving us the expression:

\[x + 20\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following statements will form an algebraic expression?
options: The difference of two numbers is equal to \[4\].
The difference of two numbers gives an even number.
The sum of two numbers is equal to \[8\].
The sum of two numbers, \[x\] and \[y\].
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback: The sum of two numbers, \[x\] and \[y\] forms an algebraic expression while all other options are statements.

subject: Mathematics
q: Ali has Rs. \[x\] and his friend has Rs. \[10\]. What is the algebraic expression for the total money they both have?
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback:
We simply add the amount of money both friends have which will give us the required expression:

\[x + 10\]

subject: Mathematics
q: For a statement to be true the left hand side must _____
options: be equal to right hand side of the equation.
not be equal to right hand side of the equation.
must be two variables in the equation.
skill: Formation of an algebraic expression
feedback: For a statement to be true, the left hand side must be equal to the right hand side of the equation.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Write the coefficient of \[x\] from the expression:

\[3x^3 + 4x^2 + 2x\]
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback:
The number with a variable is called the coefficient of the variable.

The coefficient of \[x\] \[= 2\]

Similarly, the coefficient of \[x^3 = 3\]

and \[x^2 = 4\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Find the variables and constants in the following expression:

\[x + y + z\]
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback: This expression has three variables, \[= x, y, z\] and\[x + y + z\] no constants.

subject: Mathematics
q: In like terms, variables and powers should be the same but _____ can be different.
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback:
In like terms, variables and powers should be the same but coefficients can be different.

\[7v^4\] and \[9v^4\] are like terms with same variables and their exponents but with different coefficients.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the terms are not the same, they are called _____ terms.
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback:
If the terms are not the same, they are called unlike terms.

In \[7x^3\], \[8xy^3\], and \[10x^3\], \[7x^3\] and \[10x^3\]. Also, \[8xy^3\] and \[7x^3\] are unlike terms.

subject: Mathematics
q: Like terms are those that have the same variables and the same _____.
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback: Like terms are those that have the same variables and the same exponents. In \[7x^3\], \[8xy^3\], and \[10x^3\], \[7x^3\] and \[10x^3\] are like terms.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number appearing with the variables is called a _____.
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback:
The number appearing with the variables is called a coefficient.

In \[5x\], \[5\] is the coefficient. Similarly, in \[7y -6\], \[7\] is the coefficient.

subject: Mathematics
q: The parts of an expression connected by \[+\] and \[-\] are called the _____ of the expression.
skill: Terms, coefficients, like and unlike terms
feedback:
The parts of an expression connected by \[+\] and \[-\] are called the terms of an expression.

In \[3x + 5y + 8z - 4\]; there are \[4\] terms.

subject: Mathematics
q: An equation of the form \[ax^2+bx+c\] where \[a\neq0\] is called the _____ equation.
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial that has a degree of one is called a linear polynomial.

A polynomial that has a degree of two is called a quadratic polynomial.

Similarly, the third degree and fourth degree polynomials are called cubic and biquadratic polynomials respectively.

The degree of the given polynomial is \[2\], therefore, it is a quadratic polynomial.

subject: Mathematics
q: \[\sqrt{5}\] is a polynomial of degree _____.
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
The degree of a term is determined by the power of the variable part.

Since \[\sqrt{5}\] has no variable part, we can say that it has a variable with power zero, that is \[\sqrt{5}x^0\], because \[x^0=1\]. Thus, the degree of \[\sqrt{5}\] is zero.

subject: Mathematics
q: Select the linear binomial.
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial that has a degree of one is called a linear polynomial.

A polynomial that has two terms is called a binomial since bi means two.

Therefore, \[\frac{1}{2}y+\frac{22}{7}\] is a linear binomial, \[xy-7\] is a quadratic binomial, \[\pi x^2\] is a quadratic monomial, and \[\frac{6x^3}{x^2}\] is a linear monomial.

subject: Mathematics
q: The coefficient of \[x\] in the expansion of \[\left(x+y\right)^2\] is _____.
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
Recall that, \[\left(x+y\right)^2=x^2+2xy+y^2\]

Thus, the coefficient of \[x\] is \[1\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following is a polynomial?
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of one or more terms where the exponents of the variables are either zero or a positive integer.

Therefore, \[a^2+\frac{4a^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\sqrt{a}}\] is a polynomial because it can be simplified to:
\[a^2+\frac{4a^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\sqrt{a}}\]

\[=a^2+\frac{4a^{\frac{3}{2}}}{a^{\frac{1}{2}}}\]

\[=a^2+4a^{\frac{3}{2}-\frac{1}{2}}\]

\[=a^2+4a\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Biquadratic algebraic expression is a polynomial of degree _____.
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial that has a degree of one is called a linear polynomial.

A polynomial that has a degree of two is called a quadratic polynomial.

Similarly, polynomials that have degrees of three and four are called cubic and biquadratic polynomials respectively.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Classify the given polynomial with respect to degree.

\[8x^4+9x^3-10x^2+10\]
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial that has a degree of one is called a linear polynomial.

A polynomial that has a degree of two is called a quadratic polynomial.

Similarly, polynomials that have degrees of three and four are called cubic and biquadratic polynomials respectively.

The degree of the given polynomial is \[4\], therefore, it is a biquadratic polynomial.

skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial that has only one term is called a monomial since mono means one.

A polynomial that has two terms is called a 'binomial' since bi means two.

If there are three terms in an expression, we call it a 'trinomial' since tri means three.

For expressions with more than three terms, we usually use the word 'polynomial' since poly means many.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following is a polynomial?
skill: Define polynomials and recognise a polynomial of various variables and degrees
feedback:
A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of one or more terms where the exponents of the variables are either zero or a positive integer. Therefore, \[2c^{\frac{4}{2}}-5b+\frac{6}{7}\] is a polynomial because it can be simplified to \[2c^2-5b+\frac{6}{7}\].

\[7x^2+\sqrt{x}\] is not a polynomial because the power of the variable \[x\] in the second term is equal to \[\frac{1}{2}\], which does not belong to the set of whole numbers.

Similarly, \[\frac{10}{w}-5\] is not a polynomial because it can be written as \[10w^{-1}-5\]. The exponent of the variable \[w\] is \[-1\], which does not belong to the set of whole numbers.

q: Asif bought a pack of \[(a-b)\] pens at a rate of Rs. \[(a+b)\] per pen. If he originally had Rs. \[(a^2+4ab+4b^2)\], how much money does he have left after buying the pens?
skill: Simplify algebraic expressions involving addition, subtraction and multiplication
feedback:
If one pen costs Rs. \[(a+b)\], a pack of \[(a-b)\] pens costs Rs. \[(a-b)(a+b)\]. The amount of money he has left can be calculated as follows:

\begin{align}(a^2+4ab+4b^2)-\left[(a-b)(a+b)\right]&= a^2+4ab+4b^2-\left[a(a+b)-b(a+b)\right]\\\\&= a^2+4ab+4b^2-(a^2+ab-ba-b^2)\\\\&= a^2+4ab+4b^2-a^2-ab+ba-b^2\\\\&= a^2-a^2+4ab+4b^2-b^2\\\\&= 4ab+3b^2\end{align}

q: Calculate \[52\times61\] by using an appropriate formula.
skill: Recognize and verify: (x + a)(x + b) = x^2 + (a + b)x + ab
feedback:
Note that \[52\] can be written as \[50+2\] and \[61\] can be written as \[50+11\]. We can use the algebraic identity \[(x+a)(x+b)=x^2+(a+b)x+ab\;\] to evaluate \[52\times61\].

Here \[x=50, a=2,\] and \[b=11\].

\begin{align} 52\times61 &= (50+2)(50+11) \\ &= (50)^2+(2+11)(50)+(2)(11) \\ &= 2{,}500+(13)(50)+22 \\ &= 2{,}500+650+22 \\ &= 3{,}172 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Solve: \[(x+7)\left(x+\frac{4}{9}\right)\]
skill: Recognize and verify: (x + a)(x + b) = x^2 + (a + b)x + ab
feedback:
We can use the algebraic identity \[(x+a)(x+b)=x^2+(a+b)x+ab\;\] to solve \[(x+7)\left(x+\frac{4}{9}\right)\].

Here \[a=7\] and \[b=\frac{4}{9}\].

\begin{align} (x+7)\left(x+\frac{4}{9}\right) &= x^2+\left(7+\frac{4}{9}\right)x+(7)\left(\frac{4}{9}\right) \\\\ &= x^2+\left(\frac{63+4}{9}\right)x+\frac{28}{9} \\\\ &= x^2+\left(\frac{67}{9}\right)x+\frac{28}{9} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Solve: \[\left(x+\frac{1}{4}\right)(x+4)\]
skill: Recognize and verify: (x + a)(x + b) = x^2 + (a + b)x + ab
feedback:
We can use the algebraic identity \[(x+a)(x+b)=x^2+(a+b)x+ab\;\] to solve \[\left(x+\frac{1}{4}\right)(x+4)\].

Here \[a=\frac{1}{4}\] and \[b=4\].

\begin{align} \left(x+\frac{1}{4}\right)(x+4) &= x^2+\left(\frac{1}{4}+4\right)x+\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)(4) \\\\ &= x^2+\left(\frac{1+16}{4}\right)x+1 \\\\ &= x^2+\left(\frac{17}{4}\right)x+1 \end{align}

Here \[x=y, a=5,\] and \[b=3\].

\begin{align} (y+5)(y+3) &= y^2+(5+3)y+(5)(3) \\ &= y^2+8y+15 \end{align}

q: If \[x+\frac{1}{x}=p\], find the value of \[x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\] in terms of \[p\].
skill: Recognize and verify: (a + b)^2 = (a + b)(a + b) = a^2 + 2ab + b^2
feedback:
We can use the algebraic identity \[(a+b)^2=a^2+2ab+b^2\] to find \[\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2\].

\begin{align}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2&= x^2+2(x)\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)+ \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^2\\\\p^2&= x^2+2+\frac{1}{x^2}\\\\p^2-2&= x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\end{align}

So, \[x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}=p^2-2\]

q: What is the length of the sides of the following square?
skill: Recognize and verify: (a − b)^2 = (a − b)(a − b) = a^2 − 2ab + b^2
feedback:
Area of a square \[=\] Length\[^2\]

If the length of the sides of a square is \[a-b\], then its area is \[a^2-2ab+b^2\]. Therefore, we can compare \[x^2z^2-\frac{xz}{y}+\frac{1}{4y^2}\] with the expanded form of the algebraic identity \[(a-b)^2=a^2-2ab+b^2\]:

\[a^2-2ab+b^2= x^2z^2-\frac{xz}{y}+\frac{1}{4y^2}\]

So:

\begin{align}&a^2= x^2z^2&\Rightarrow a=xz\\\\&b^2=\frac{1}{4y^2}&\Rightarrow b=\frac{1}{2y}\end{align}

Hence, the length of the sides of the square is \[xz-\frac{1}{2y}\].

q: Find the coefficient of \[ab\] for the expanded form of \[(4a-8b)^2\].

skill: Factorize an algebraic expression by using algebraic identities
feedback:
There are three terms in the expression \[ 49a^2-14ab^2+b^4\], of which the terms \[49a^2\] and \[b^4\] are perfect squares.
The negative middle term, \[-14ab^2\], is twice the product of the square roots of the other two terms. Therefore, this expression can be compared with the algebraic identity \[(a-b)^2=a^2-2ab+b^2\].

\begin{align} 49a^2-14ab^2+b^4&=(7a)^2-2(7a)(b^2)+(b^2)^2 \\\\ &= (7a-b^2)^2\\\\ &=(7a-b^2)(7a-b^2) \end{align}

q: Factorise \[(x^6-y^2)\].

\begin{align} x^6-y^2&= (x^3)^2-(y)^2\\\\ &= (x^3+y)(x^3-y) \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Factorise \[x^4-16y^4\] completely.
skill: Factorize an algebraic expression by using algebraic identities
feedback:
There are two terms in \[ x^4-16y^4\], both of which are perfect squares. Therefore, this expression can be compared with the algebraic identity \[ a^2-b^2=(a+b)(a-b)\].

\begin{align} x^4-16y^4&=(x^2)^2-(4y^2)^2 \\\\ &= (x^2-4y^2)(x^2+4y^2) \end{align}

\[x^2-4y^2\] can be factorised further by using the identity \[ a^2-b^2=(a+b)(a-b)\].

\begin{align} (x^2-4y^2)(x^2+4y^2)&= [x^2-(2y)^2](x^2+4y^2)\\\\ &= (x+2y)(x-2y)(x^2+4y^2) \end{align}

q: Factorise \[c^8+12c^4d+36d^2\].

feedback:
There are three terms in the expression \[ c^8+12c^4d+36d^2\] of which the terms \[c^8\] and \[36d^2\] are perfect squares. The middle term, \[12c^4d \], is twice the product of the square roots of the other two terms. Therefore, this expression can be compared with the algebraic identity \[(a+b)^2=a^2+2ab+b^2\].

\begin{align} c^8+12c^4d+36d^2&=(c^4)^2+2(c^4)(6d)+(6d)^2 \\\\ &= (c^4+6d)^2\\\\ &=(c^4+6d)(c^4+6d) \end{align}

q: Ali and Zaheer scored \[24\] goals together in a football league. Ali scored \[4\] more goals than Zaheer. Convert the given statements into simultaneous linear equations using \[a\] and \[z\] as the two unknown variables.
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
Let the number of Ali's goals \[= a\]
Let the number of Zaheer's goals \[= z\]

The first statement says that Ali and Zaheer score \[24\] goals together. This means that if we add the number of goals both of them scored, we will get \[24\]:
\[a + z = 24 \] ________(\[i\])

The second statement says that Ali scored \[4\] more goals than Zaheer. This means that if we add \[4\] to Zaheer's number of goals, we get the number of goals that Ali scored:
\[z + 4 = a \ \ OR \ \ a = z + 4 \] ________(\[ii\])

subject: Mathematics
q: One chocolate and one candy cost Rs. \[30\] while \[3\] chocolates and \[4\] candies cost Rs. 100. Assuming \[c\] is the cost of one chocolate and \[d\] is the cost of one candy, which of the following simultaneous equations represent the statement above?
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
The cost of one chocolate = Rs. \[c\]
The cost of one candy = Rs. \[d\]

One chocolate and one candy cost Rs. \[30\] in total. This statement can be written as:
\[c + d = 30 \] ________(\[i\]) This becomes our first equation.

The cost of \[3\] chocolates and \[4\] candies is Rs. \[100\]. In equation form, it can be written like this:
\[3c + 4d = 100 \] ________(\[ii\]) This becomes our second equation.

subject: Mathematics
q: If we add twice the age of Zubair to the age of his mother, we get \[60\]. But when we add twice the age of his mother to Zubair's age, we get 90. If we assume Zubair's age to be \[z\] and his mother's age to be \[m\], which \[2\] simultaneous equations represent these \[2\] statements?
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
If we assume Zubair's age \[= z\], and his mother's age \[= m\], then the statement "add twice the age of Zubair to his mother's age, we get \[60\]" becomes:

\begin{align} 2 \times z + m &= 60 \\ 2z + m &= 60 \\ \end{align}

This becomes our first equation.

The second statement says "when we add twice the age of his mother to Zubair's age we get \[90\]." In equation form we can write it as:

\begin{align} \mbox{twice the age of the mother} + \mbox{Zubair's age } = 90 \\ 2 \times m + z = 90 \\ 2m + z = 90 \\ \end{align}

This becomes our second equation.

subject: Mathematics
q: Shadab Khan conceded \[30\] runs in his assigned  \[10\] overs. Assuming that he gave away \[x\] runs per over, put the given information into equation form.
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
Shadab gave \[x\] runs per over for his \[10\] overs. So, the total runs he conceded will be equal to \[x \times 10\]. We know that the total runs he gave away were \[30\]. These two statements combine to give us the following equation:

\[x \times 10 = 30\]
\[10x = 30\]

Let Fakhar Zaman's score = \[y\]

We know that their partnership was worth \[122\] runs in total and that there were no extras from the bowlers. This means that the total of their individual scores was \[122\]. This can be written in equation form as:

\[x + y = 122\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
Jawad has \[2\] mobile phones. The number of songs in the first phone is double the number of songs in the other. Create a linear equation in \[2\] variables that relates the number of songs in both phones.
Let \[x\] be the number of songs in the first phone.
Let \[y\] be the number of songs in the second phone.
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
The first step is to assign variables to the unknown quantities. As provided,
Let \[x\] be the number of songs in the first phone.
Let \[y\] be the number of songs in the second phone.

The statement says that the number of songs in the first phone (\[x\]) is double the number of songs in the other phone (\[y\]). This means that \[x\] is two times the number of songs in the other phone (\[y\]). In equation form this is written as:

\begin{align} x &= 2 \ of \ y \\ x &= 2 \times y \\ x &= 2y \\ \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q:
The general form of linear equations in two variables is:
\[ax + by = c\], where \[x\] can be any variable.
Convert the following linear equation into the general form and identify the values of \[a\], \[b\], and \[c\].

\[3y + 2x -27 = x - 2y\]
skill: Recognize and know the formation of simultaneous linear equations in one and two variables
feedback:
To identify the values of \[a\], \[b\], and \[c\], let us convert the given equation to the general form:

\begin{align} 3y + 2x - 27 &= x - 2y \\ 3y + 2y + 2x - x - 27 &= 0\\ 5y + x &= 27 \\ x + 5y &= 27 \\ \end{align}

After converting the given equation into its general form, we can identify the values of \[a\], \[b\], and \[c\] to be:
\[a = 1\], \[b = 5\], and \[c = 27\]

q:
How many solutions does the following equation have?

\[\frac{x-5}{4}=7\]
skill: Define and solve a linear equation in one variable of the type: ax + b = c
feedback:
A linear equation in one variable has exactly one solution.
The equation \[\frac{x-5}{4}=7\] is a linear equation in one variable. So, it has exactly one solution.

\begin{align} \frac{x-5}{4} &= 7 \\\\ x-5 &= 7\times4 \\\\ x-5&=28\\\\ x&= 28+5\\\\ x&= 33 \end{align}

q: A father distributes money in such a way that the elder brother gets six times as much money as the younger one. If the total money to be distributed is Rs.\[6,300\]. How much does the younger brother get?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
Total money to be distributed \[= 6300\]

Let the amount the younger brother gets be \[=\] Rs. \[y\]

Elder brother gets six times more than younger brother \[=\] Rs. \[6y\]

Total money \[=\] Younger brother's share \[+\] Elder brother's share

\[6300 = y + 6y\]

\[6300 = 7y\]

\[7y = 6300\]

\[y = \frac{6300}{7}\]

\[y = 900\]

Therefore, the younger brother gets Rs. \[900\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Ramsha buys a book and a bag for a total of Rs. \[470\]. If the book costs Rs.\[50\] more than twice the cost of the bag, what is the price of the bag?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
Total cost of bag and book \[=\] Rs. \[470\]

Let the cost of the bag be \[= x\]

Then, cost of book\[= 2x + 50\]

Total cost \[=\] cost of the book \[+\] cost of the bag

\[470 = x + \left(2x + 50 \right)\]

\[x + 2x + 50 = 470\]

\[3x + 50 = 470\]

\[3x = 420\]

\[x = \frac{420}{3}\]

\[x = 140\]

Therefore, the price of the bag is Rs.\[140\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Saad is \[4\] times as old as Ali. The sum of their ages is \[45\] years. What is Saad's age?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
Let's suppose Ali's age is \[= x\] years

Then Saad's age \[= 4x\] years

Sum of their ages \[=45\] years

Sum of ages \[=\] Saad's age \[+\] Ali's age

\[45 = 4x + x\]

\[5x = 45\]

\[x = \frac{45}{5}\]

\[x = 9\]

\[x\] is Ali's age, so we need to multiply this by \[4\] to calculate Saad's age.

Saad's age \[= 4\times9\]

Saad's age \[= 36\] years

subject: Mathematics
q: Ali buys a football and a tennis ball for Rs. \[800\]. The football costs \[150\] rupees more than the tennis ball. What is the cost of the football?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
Cost of the football and tennis ball \[= 800\]

Supposed cost of a tennis ball \[= x\]

Cost of a football \[= x + 150\]

So, the cost of a football \[+\] tennis ball \[= x + \left(x + 150\right)\]

Total cost \[=\] Cost of the football \[+\] Cost of the tennis ball

\[800 = x + \left(x + 150\right)\]

\[800 = x + x + 150\]

\[800 = 2x + 150\]

\[2x + 150 = 800\]

\[2x = 800 \ - 150\]

\[2x = 650\]

\[x = \frac{650}{2}\]

\[x = 325\]

So, cost of the tennis ball \[=\] Rs. \[325\]

Cost of the football \[= 325 + 150\]

\[=\] Rs. \[475\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The base of an isosceles triangle is double the length of the other two sides.  The perimetre of the triangle is \[20\] cm. What is the length of the base?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
We know that the perimetre of the triangle \[= 20\] cm.

We know that the base is twice the length of the other two sides. We also know that the other two sides are equal in length, since in an isosceles triangle the two sides other than the base are equal.

In this isosceles triangle, let's consider the lengths, other than base, as \[= x\] cm.

Base of triangle = \[2x\] cm

Perimetre \[= \mbox{side 1} + \mbox{side 2} + \mbox{base}\]

Perimetre \[= x + x + 2x\]

\[20 = x + x + 2x\]

\[4x = 20\]

\[x = \frac{20}{4}\]

\[x = 5\]

The length of the base is double so \[= 2x\]

\[= 2\times5\]

\[= 10\] cm

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of three consecutive numbers is \[24\]. What is the value of the smallest number?
skill: Solve real life problems involving linear equations
feedback:
Since we do not know any of the three consecutive numbers, we suppose the first and the smallest of the three numbers is \[x\].

The second number will be \[ x + 1\].

The third number will be \[ x + 2\].

The three numbers are \[x\], \[x + 1\], and \[x + 2\] as they are consecutive numbers.

Sum of the three numbers \[= 20\]

So, \[ x + \left(x + 1\right) + \left(x + 2\right) = 20\]

\[ x + x + 1 + x + 2 = 20\]

Grouping the like terms, \[ 3x + 3 = 24\]

\[3x = 24 - 3\]

\[x = \frac{21}{3}\]

\[x = 7\]

So, the smallest number is \[7\]

The next number is \[7 + 1 = 8\]

The largest number is \[7 + 2 = 9\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the area of the given composite figure.
skill: Solve real-life problems related to the area and perimeter of composite figures
feedback:
The given composite figure is composed of two basic geometric shapes: a triangle and a trapezium.
To find its area, we will first calculate the areas of the triangle and the trapezium separately and then add them up.

\begin{align} \mbox{Area of the triangle} &= \frac{1}{2} \times \mbox{Base} \times \mbox{Height} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2} \times 7 \times 5 \\\\ &= \frac{35}{2} \\\\ &= 17.5 \mbox{ ft}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the trapezium} &= \frac{1}{2} \times \mbox{Height} \times \mbox{Sum of the lengths of parallel sides} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times (5+8) \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 13 \\\\ &= \frac{65}{2} \\ &= 32.5 \mbox{ ft}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the composite figure} &= \mbox{Area of the triangle} + \mbox{Area of the trapezium} \\ &= 17.5+32.5\\ &= 50 \mbox{ ft}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: A running track is made up of a rectangle and two semicircles. If Sara completes three rounds of the track, how many metres did she run?
skill: Solve real-life problems related to the area and perimeter of composite figures
feedback:
Since the perimeter of any shape is the length of its outer boundary, we add the lengths of all the lines that make up the boundary.
We first need to calculate the circumference of the circle, since it makes up the curved sides of the track.

\begin{align} C_\mbox{circle} &= 2 \pi r \\ &= 2 \times 3.14 \times 20 \\ &= 125.6 \mbox{ m} \\\\ \mbox{Perimeter} &= 125.6+50+50 \\ &= 225.6 \mbox{ m} \end{align}

Since Sara completes three rounds of the track, we will multiply the perimeter of the track with \[3\].

\[3 \times 225.6 = 676.8\] m

Thus, Sara runs \[676.8\] metres.

subject: Mathematics
q: A semicircle is cut out from a rectangle. If the area of the semicircle was \[175\] m\[^2\], what is the area of the shaded part?

feedback: \begin{align} \mbox{Area of the shaded region} &= \mbox{Area of the rectangle} - \mbox{Area of the semicircle} \\ &= (l \times w) - 175 \\ &= (26 \times 10) - 175 \\ &= 260 - 175 \\ &= 85 \mbox{ m}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the area of the shaded region if the radius of each circle is \[r\], the length of the rectangle is \[l\], and the width of the rectangle is \[w\].

feedback: \begin{align} \mbox{Area of the shaded region} &= \mbox{Area of the rectangle} - 3(\mbox{Area of the circle}) \\ &= (l \times w) - 3 (\pi r^2) \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The given figure is composed of two congruent parallelograms. Find its perimeter.

feedback:
Since the perimeter of any shape is the length of its outer boundary, we add the lengths of all the lines that make up the boundary.
We know that the opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal in measure.



\begin{align} \mbox{Perimeter} &= 10+4+4+10+4+4 \\ &= 36 \mbox{ cm} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the area of the coloured region.
skill: Solve real-life problems related to the area and perimeter of composite figures
feedback:
To find the area of the coloured region, we will first calculate the areas of the rectangle and the triangle separately and then subtract the area of the triangle from the area of the rectangle.

\begin{align} \mbox{Area of the rectangle} &= \mbox{Length} \times \mbox{Width} \\ &= 25 \times 15 \\ &= 375 \mbox{ cm}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the triangle} &= \frac{1}{2} \times \mbox{Base} \times \mbox{Height} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2} \times 25 \times 10 \\\\ &= 25 \times 5 \\ &= 125 \mbox{ cm}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the shaded region} &= \mbox{Area of the rectangle} - \mbox{Area of the triangle} \\ &= 375-125\\ &= 250 \mbox{ cm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: A square is inscribed in a circle. If the radius of the circle is \[r\] and the length of each side of the square is \[l\], find the area of the purple region.
skill: Solve real-life problems related to the area and perimeter of composite figures
feedback: \begin{align} \mbox{Area of the purple region} &= \mbox{Area of the circle} - \mbox{Area of the square} \\ &= \pi r^2 - l^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the area of the given composite figure?
skill: Solve real-life problems related to the area and perimeter of composite figures
feedback:
The given composite figure is composed of two basic geometric shapes: a circle and a rectangle.
The area of two semicircles is equal to the area of one full circle. To find the area of the given figure, we will first calculate the areas of the circle and the rectangle separately and then add them up.

\begin{align} \mbox{Radius of the circle} &= \frac{\mbox{Diameter}}{2} \\\\ r &= \frac{4}{2} \\\\ r &= 2 \mbox{ mm} \\\\ \mbox{Area of the circle} &= \pi r^2 \\ &= 3.14 \times 2^2\\ &= 3.14 \times 4 \\ &= 12.56 \mbox{ mm}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the rectangle} &= \mbox{Length} \times \mbox{Width} \\ &= 7 \times 4 \\ &= 28 \mbox{ mm}^2 \\\\ \mbox{Area of the composite figure} &= \mbox{Area of the circle} + \mbox{Area of the rectangle} \\ &= 12.56+28\\ &= 40.56 \mbox{ mm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: You are a scientist who has helped Pakistan send a remote-controlled rover to the moon. You want the rover to go completely around the moon ,at its equator, and return to where it started. If the radius of the moon at the equator is taken as \[2{,}100\] km, what is the distance the rover will have to cover to go around the moon once? (Use \[\pi = \frac{22}{7})\])
skill: Use π to find the circumference and area a circular region using formula
feedback:
To find the distance the rover will have to travel to go around the moon once, we need to calculate the circumference of the moon at the equator using the equatorial radius given in the question.

\begin{align} \mbox{Distance to be covered} &= \mbox{Circumference of the moon at the equator} \\ &= 2\pi r_{moon}\\ \\ &= 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 2{,}100 \\ \\ &= 2 \times 22 \times 300 \\ &= 44 \times 300 \\ &= 13{,}200 \mbox{ km} \\ \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The diameter of Amna's bicycle wheel is \[14\] cm. If it takes \[150\] revolutions of her bicycle wheel to cover the distance of her street, calculate the length of her street? (Use \[\pi = \frac{22}{7}\])
skill: Use π to find the circumference and area a circular region using formula
feedback:
When we calculate the circumference of the wheel, we get the distance the wheel covers in one revolution. So, if we multiply it with the total number of revolutions it takes to get from one end of the street to the other, we will get the length of the street.

First, we calculate the circumference of the wheel:
\[C = \pi d = \frac{22}{7} \times 14 = 44 \mbox{ cm} \]

Now, to find the length of the street, we multiply the circumference of the wheel with the number of revolutions it took to cover the street:

\begin{align} \mbox{Total distance} &= \mbox{Circumference of wheel} \times \mbox{Number of revolutions} \\ &= 44 \times 150 \\ &= 6{,}600 \mbox{ cm} \\ \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If the circumference of a given circle is \[50.27\] cm, what is its radius?
skill: Use π to find the circumference and area a circular region using formula
feedback:
We can calculate the radius of a circle when its circumference is given using this formula:

\begin{align} c &= 2 \pi r \\ \\ r &= \frac{c}{2\pi} \\ \\ r &= \frac{50.27}{2\times 3.14} \\ \\ r &= 8.00 \mbox{ cm} \\ \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The diameter of the Earth at the equator is \[12{,}742\] km. Calculate the circumference of the Earth at the equator. Use \[\pi \approx 3.14\].

The circumference of the Earth at the equator can be calculated using the formula for the circumference of a circle:

\begin{align} c &= \pi d \\ \\ &= 3.14 \times 12{,}742 \\ \\ &= 40{,}009 \mbox{ km} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics

subject: Mathematics
q: The formula to calculate the area of a circle is _____.
skill: Use π to find the circumference and area a circular region using formula
feedback:
The formula to calculate the area of a circle is \[\pi r^2\], where \[r\] is the radius of the circle.

\[2 \pi r\] is the formula used to calculate the circumference of a circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: Given the dimensions of the right triangular prism, calculate its surface area in square metres.
skill: Calculate the volume and surface area of right prisms and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The triangular face is the base of the prism. The base of the container is a triangle with lengths \[20\] cm, \[80\] cm and \[50\] cm. Its height is \[30\] cm. To calculate total surface area, first we will calculate the perimeter of the base and the base area. Then we will insert the values in the formula to calculate the total surface area. Finally, we will convert the total surface area from cm\[^2\] to m\[^2\].

\begin{align} \mbox{Total surface area}&= \mbox{Perimeter of the base} \times \mbox{Height} + 2 \times \mbox{Base area}\\\\ \mbox{Perimeter of the base}&= \mbox {Side} + \mbox {Side} + \mbox {Side}\\ &= 20 + 50 + 80\\ &=150 \mbox{ cm}\\\\ \mbox{Base area }&=\frac{1}{2}\times \mbox{Base} \times \mbox{Height}\\\\ &=\frac{1}{2}\times 20 \times 50\\\\ &= 500 \mbox{ cm}^2\\\\ \mbox{Total surface area}&= 150 \times{30} + 2 \left(500\right)\\ &=4{,}500 + 1{,}000\\ &= 5{,}500 \mbox{ cm}^2\\ 1\mbox{ m}^2&= 10{,}000\mbox{ cm}^2\\ 5{,}500\mbox{ cm}^2&=\frac{5{,}500}{10{,}000}\\\\ &= 0.55 \mbox{ m}^2 \end{align}

The surface area of the triangular prism is \[0.55\] m\[^2\].

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the volume of the following prism in cubic centimetres.
skill: Calculate the volume and surface area of right prisms and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The base of the prism is a trapezium with parallel sides measuring \[4\] m and \[8\] m. Height of the base is \[2\] m. To calculate the volume, first we will calculate the base area. Then we will insert the values in the formula to calculate the volume. Finally, we will convert the total volume from m\[^3\] to cm\[^3\].

\begin{align} \mbox{Volume of a prism }&=\mbox{Base area} \times \mbox{Height} \\\\ \mbox{Trapezoidal base area }&=\frac{1}{2}\times \mbox{Sum of parallel sides} \times \mbox{Height}\\\\ &=\frac{1}{2}\times \left(4 + 8\right) \times 2\\\\ &=\frac{1}{2}\times 12 \times 2\\ &= 12\mbox{ m}^2\\\\ \mbox{Volume }&=12 \times 18\\ &= 216 \mbox{ m}^3\\\\ 1\mbox{ m}^3&= 1{,}000{,}000 \mbox{ cm}^3\\ 216\mbox { m}^3&= 216 \times 1{,}000{,}000\\ &= 216{,}000{,}000\mbox{ cm}^3\\ \end{align}

The volume of the prism is \[216{,}000{,}000\] cm\[^{3}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: A container has a length of \[5\] m, a height of \[8\] m and a width of \[3\] m. Find its surface area in square centimetres.
skill: Calculate the volume and surface area of right prisms and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The base of the container is a rectangle with length \[5\] metres and breadth \[3\] metres. To calculate total surface area, first we will calculate the perimeter of the base and the base area. Then we will insert the values in the formula to calculate the total surface area. Finally, we will convert the total surface area from m\[^2\] to cm\[^2\].

\begin{align} \mbox{Total surface area}&= \mbox{Perimeter of the base} \times \mbox{Height} + 2 \times \mbox{Base area}\\\\ \mbox{Perimeter of the base}&= 2 \times \left(\mbox{Length} + \mbox{Breadth} \right)\\ &= 2 \times \left(5 + 3\right)\\ &=16 \mbox{ m}\\\\ \mbox{Base area}&= \mbox{Length} \times \mbox{Breadth}\\ &=5 \times 3\\ &= 15 \mbox{ m}^2\\\\ \mbox{Total surface area}&= 16 \times\mbox{8} + 2 \left(15\right)\\ &=16 \times 8 + 30\\ &=128 + 30\\ &=158 \mbox{ m}^2\\ 1\mbox{ m}^2&= 10,000 \mbox{ cm}^2\\ 158\mbox { m}&= 158\times 10,000\\ &= 1,580,000\mbox{ cm}^2\\ \end{align}

The surface area of the container is \[1{,}580{,}000\] cm\[^2\].

subject: Mathematics
q: A building of length \[12\] m and breadth \[8\] m has a total surface area of \[992\] m\[^{2}\]. Find its height and its volume.
skill: Calculate the volume and surface area of right prisms and solve related real life problems
feedback:
To calculate the volume of the building, we need to calculate its base area and height. Since we don't know the height, we can calculate it by using the given surface area. Then we will use it to calculate the volume.

\begin{align} \mbox{Total surface area}&= (\mbox{Perimeter of the base} \times \mbox{Height}) + (2 \times \mbox{Base area})\\\\ \mbox{Perimeter of the base}&= 2 \times \left(\mbox{Length} + \mbox{Breadth} \right)\\ &= 2 \times \left(12 + 8\right)\\ &=40 \mbox{ m}\\\\ \mbox{Base area}&= \mbox{Length} \times \mbox{Breadth}\\ &=12 \times 8\\ &= 96 \mbox{ m}^2\\\\ \mbox{Total surface area}&= 40 \times\mbox{Height} + 2 \left(96\right)\\ 992&=40 \times \mbox{Height} + 192\\ 992 - 192 &=40 \times \mbox{Height}\\ 800&=40 \times \mbox{Height}\\ \mbox{Height} &=\frac{800}{40}\\\\ &= 20\mbox{ m}\\\\ \mbox{Volume }&=\mbox{Base area} \times \mbox{Height} \\ &= 96 \times 20\\ &= 1{,}920\mbox{ m}^3 \end{align}

The height of the rectangular building is \[20\] metres, and its volume is \[1{,}920\] m\[^3\].

subject: Mathematics
q: A tent is shaped like a triangular prism. Its volume is \[196\] cm\[^{3}\]. Given the dimensions of its base, calculate its height.
skill: Calculate the volume and surface area of right prisms and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The tent is a triangular prism.

\begin{align} \mbox{Volume of a prism }&=\mbox{Base area} \times \mbox{Height} \\\\ \mbox{Base area }&=\frac{1}{2}\times \mbox{Base} \times \mbox{Height}\\\\ &=\frac{1}{2}\times \mbox{4} \times \mbox{7}\\\\ &= 14 \mbox{ cm}^2\\\\ \mbox{Volume }&=\mbox{Base area} \times \mbox{Height} \\ 196&=14\times \mbox{Height} \\\\ \mbox{Height} &= \frac{196}{14}\\\\ \mbox{Height} &= 14 \mbox{ cm} \end{align}

The height of the tent is \[14\] cm.

subject: Mathematics
q: A long open steel pipe is \[21\] m long and has a radius of \[1\] m. What is its surface area? (Use \[\pi = \frac{22}{7}\])
topic: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinder
skill: Find the surface area of a cylinder and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The formula for surface area of a cylinder is: \[2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h\]. As the pipe is open from both ends, the first part of the formula \[2 \pi r^2\], that gives the surface area of the circular surfaces on either end of the cylinder, becomes \[0\]. Therefore, the surface area of the pipe becomes \[2 \pi r h\].

The length of the pipe is merely the height of the cylinder in our formula:
Radius \[= 1\] m
Height \[= 21\] m.

\begin{align} \mbox{Surface Area} &= 2 \pi r h \\\\ &= 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 1 \times 21 \\\\ &=132 \ \mbox{m}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the surface area of a cylindrical drinking glass which has a radius of \[3.5\] cm and a height of \[7\] cm? (Use \[\pi = \frac{22}{7}\])
topic: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinder
skill: Find the surface area of a cylinder and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The formula for surface area of a cylinder is: \[2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h\]. We need to consider that the glass only has the bottom surface. The top of the glass is open. So, in the first term of the formula the \[2 \pi r^2\] representing two circular surfaces becomes just \[\pi r^2\]to represent one circular bottom. The solution will now be:

Radius \[= 3.5\] cm. Height \[= 7\] cm.

\begin{align} \mbox{Surface Area} &= \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h \\ &= \left(\frac{22}{7} \times 3.5^2\right) + \left(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 3.5 \times 7\right) \\ &= 38.5 + 154 \\ &=192.5 \ \mbox{cm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the surface area of the given cylinder? (Use \[\pi = \frac{22}{7}\])
topic: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinder
skill: Find the surface area of a cylinder and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The formula for surface area of a cylinder is: \[2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h\].

Radius \[= 1\] cm. Height \[= 7\] cm.

\begin{align} \mbox{Surface Area} &= 2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h \\ &= \left(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 1^2\right) + \left(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 1 \times 7\right) \\ &= \frac{44}{7} + 44 \\ &=50.28 \ \mbox{cm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the surface area of the following cylinder? (Use \[\pi = 3.14\])
topic: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinder
skill: Find the surface area of a cylinder and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The formula for surface area of a cylinder is: \[2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h\].

Radius \[= 1\] cm. Height \[= 1\] cm.

\begin{align} \mbox{Surface Area} &= 2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h \\ &= (2 \times 3.14 \times 1^2) + (2 \times 3.14 \times 1 \times 1) \\ & = (6.28)+(6.28)\\ &= 12.56 \ \mbox{cm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the surface area of a cylinder which has a radius of \[2\] cm and a height of \[10\] cm? (Use \[\pi = 3.14\])
topic: Surface Area and Volume of Cylinder
skill: Find the surface area of a cylinder and solve related real life problems
feedback:
The formula for surface area is: \[2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h\].

Radius \[= 2\] cm. Height \[= 10\] cm.

\begin{align} \mbox{Surface Area} &= 2 \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r h \\ &= (2 \times 3.14 \times 2^2) + (2 \times 3.14 \times 2 \times 10) \\ &= (25.12) + (125.6) \\ &= 150.72 \ \mbox{cm}^2 \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[6\: cm \] and height \[7 \: cm\]. (Take \[π\] to be \[\frac{22}{7}\] )
skill: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder whose base radius and height are given
feedback:
The volume \[V\] of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[ r\] and height h is

\[V=πr^2 h \]

Substituting \[r=6\: cm, h=7\: cm\] and \[ π=\frac{22}{7}\], we have

\[V=\frac{22}{7}×6^2×7 \]

\[=22×36 \]

\[=792\: {cm}^3\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The base radius and height of a right circular cylinder is \[ 5\: cm\] and \[8 \: cm\] respectively. If the radius increases by \[x \: cm\], its volume increases by \[312π\: {cm}^3\]. Find the value of \[x\].
skill: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder whose base radius and height are given
feedback:
The volume \[V\] of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[r\] and height \[h\] is

\[V=πr^2 h \]

Substituting \[r=5\] and \[h=8\], we have

\[V=π(5^2 )(8)=200π \: {cm}^3 \]

Since the volume increases by \[312π\: {cm}^3\] when its radius increases by \[x \: cm\], we have

\[200π+312π=π(5+x)^2 (8) \]

\[8π(x^2+10x+25)=512π \]

\[x^2+10x+25=64 \]

\[x^2+10x-39=0 \]

\[x^2-3x+13x-39=0 \]

\[x(x-3)+13(x-3)=0 \]

\[(x+13)(x-3)=0 \]

\[⇒x=-13\] or \[ x=3 \]

Since \[5-13=-8\] is negative, we reject this answer

\[⇒x=3\: cm\]

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the base radius of a right circular cylinder of volume \[V\] and height \[h\].
skill: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder whose base radius and height are given
feedback:
The volume \[V\] of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[r \] and height \[h\] is

\[V=πr^2 h \]

\[⇒r^2=\frac{V}{πh}\]

\[r=\sqrt{\frac{V}{πh}}\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The volume of a right circular cylinder is \[ V\]. If the base radius is doubled without changing the height, find its new volume.
skill: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder whose base radius and height are given
feedback:
The volume \[V\] of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[r\] and height \[h\] is

\[V=πr^2 h \]

Let its new volume be \[V_1\]. We thus have

\[V_1=π(2r)^2 h\]\[\: \: \: \]\[\: \: \: \] \[\: \] (since its base radius is doubled)

\[=4πr^2 h \]

\[=4V\]\[\: \: \: \: \: \] \[\: \: \: \: \] \[\: \: \: \]\[\: \: \: \] \[\: \: \: \] \[\: \](using \[V=πr^2 h \])

subject: Mathematics
q: Find the volume of the right circular cylinder given that the diameter of its base is \[d\] and its height is \[h\].
skill: Find the volume of a right circular cylinder whose base radius and height are given
feedback:
The volume \[V \] of a right circular cylinder of base radius \[r\] and height \[h\] is

\[V=πr^2 h \]

Since the diameter of the base is \[d\], its radius is \[\frac{d}{2}\].

Substituting \[r=\frac{d}{2}\], we have

\[V=π\left ( \frac{d}{2} \right ) ^2h \]

\[=\frac{πd^2 h}{4}\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
The diagonals of _____ are always equal.

I.   a rhombus
II.  a kite
III. a square
IV. a rectangle
skill: Quadrilaterals and their types
feedback:
The diagonals of a square and a rectangle are always equal.
The two of them are parallelograms having opposite sides parallel and equal, and every two adjacent sides make an angle of \[90^{\circ}\] with each other.



A rhombus is also a parallelogram, but its diagonals are not always equal. They're equal when its adjacent sides making an angle of \[90^{\circ}\] with each other, in which case, it's actually a square.



A kite is a quadrilateral that has two pairs of adjacent sides equal. The diagonals of a kite can be equal when all four of its sides are equal, adjacent pairs of which are perpendicular or at \[90^{\circ}\] with each other, in which case, it's simply a square; but that's generally not the case.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The diagonals of _____ are always perpendicular to each other.

I.   a rhombus
II.  a square
III. a kite
skill: Quadrilaterals and their types
feedback:
The diagonals of a rhombus, a square, and a kite are always perpendicular to each other.
A rhombus and a square are parallelograms having four equal sides, while a kite has two pairs of adjacent sides equal.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Pick the statements that are TRUE.

I.   Every rhombus is a square, but not every square is a rhombus.
II.  Every square is a rhombus, but not every rhombus is a square.
III. All rhombuses and squares are kites, but not all kites are rhombuses or squares.
skill: Quadrilaterals and their types
feedback:
A rhombus is a parallelogram having four equal sides. A square is a parallelogram having four equal sides, and all four internal angles equal to \[90^{\circ}\]. Therefore, every square is also a rhombus because of all sides being equal, but a rhombus cannot be a square unless each pair of its adjacent sides makes an angle of \[90^{\circ}\].



A kite is a quadrilateral in which the two pairs of adjacent sides are always equal. It can have all of the four sides equal, in which case, it's simply a rhombus or a square. So, all rhombuses and squares are kites because of adjacent sides being equal, but not all kites are rhombuses or squares.

subject: Mathematics
q: How many quadrilaterals are there in this figure?
skill: Quadrilaterals and their types

subject: Mathematics
q: If the measures of the three interior angles of a quadrilateral are \[136 ^{\circ}, 127^{\circ},\] and \[53^{\circ}\], find the measure of the fourth interior angle.
skill: Use properties of quadrilaterls to find unknown angles
feedback:
Since the sum of the measures of the interior angles of a quadrilateral is equal to \[360^{\circ}\], therefore,

\begin{align} m \angle A + m \angle B + m \angle C + m \angle D &= 360^{\circ} \\ 136^{\circ} + 127^{\circ} + 53^{\circ} + x &= 360^{\circ} \\ 316^{\circ} + x &= 360^{\circ} \\ x &= 360^{\circ} - 316^{\circ} \\ x &= 44^{\circ} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The figure below shows a rectangle \[ABCD\]. Find the value of angle \[AEB\].
skill: Use properties of quadrilaterls to find unknown angles
feedback:
Given that \[ABCD\] is a rectangle. All four angles in a rectangle are right angles. Thus,

\begin{align} m \angle ABE + m \angle CBE &= 90^{\circ} \\ m \angle ABE &= 90^{\circ} - m \angle CBE \\ &= 90^{\circ} - 55^{\circ} \\ &= 35^{\circ} \end{align}

Also, the sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle is equal to \[180^{\circ}\], therefore,

\begin{align} m \angle BAE + m \angle AEB + m \angle ABE &= 180^{\circ} \\ 70^{\circ} + m \angle AEB + 35^{\circ} &= 180^{\circ} \\ 105^{\circ} + m \angle AEB &= 180^{\circ} \\ m \angle AEB &= 180^{\circ} - 105^{\circ} \\ m \angle AEB &= 75^{\circ} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The figure below shows an isosceles triangle \[ABC\] such that \[m\overline{AC} = m\overline{BC}\]. Find the value of angle \[z\].
skill: Use properties of quadrilaterls to find unknown angles
feedback:
Since \[\triangle ABC\] is an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite the equal sides are also equal in measure. Thus,

\[m \angle A = m \angle B = 40^{\circ}\]

Also, the sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle is equal to \[180^{\circ}\], therefore,

\begin{align} m \angle A + m \angle B + m \angle C &= 180^{\circ} \\ 40^{\circ} + 40^{\circ} + y &= 180^{\circ} \\ 80^{\circ} + y &= 180^{\circ} \\ y &= 180^{\circ} - 80^{\circ} \\ y &= 100^{\circ} \end{align}

The sum of the measures of the interior angles of a quadrilateral is equal to \[360^{\circ}\], therefore,

\begin{align} y + z + 90^{\circ} + 78^{\circ} &= 360^{\circ} \\ 100^{\circ} + z + 90^{\circ} + 78^{\circ} &= 360^{\circ} \\ 268^{\circ} + z &= 360^{\circ} \\ z &= 360^{\circ} - 268^{\circ} \\ z &= 92^{\circ} \end{align}

Thus, \[z = 92^{\circ}\]

subject: Mathematics
q: What is the value of \[\angle D\]?
skill: Use properties of quadrilaterls to find unknown angles
feedback:
Since the sum of the measures of the interior angles of a quadrilateral is equal to \[360^{\circ}\], therefore,

\begin{align} m \angle A + m \angle B + m \angle C + m \angle D &= 360^{\circ} \\ 3x+3x+4x+2x &= 360^{\circ} \\ 12x &= 360^{\circ} \\ x &= \frac{360^{\circ}}{12} \\\\ x &= 30^{\circ} \end{align}

Thus, \[m \angle D = 2x = 60^{\circ}\]

subject: Mathematics
q: In the following diagram, which side is congruent to BC?
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback: The opposite sides of a parallelogram are congruent. In this figure, side AD is opposite to side BC, hence AD is congruent to BC.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram, the _____ bisect each other.
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback:
In a parallelogram, the diagonals bisect each other.

The opposite angles and the opposite sides never meet and never cut each other.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram, all the consecutive angles are _____.
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback:
All consecutive angles in a parallelogram are supplementary. This means that when you add those angles, they give 180 \[^\circ\].

Note that, opposite angles of a parallelogram are congruent.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a parallelogram, the \[2\] pairs of opposite angles are _____.
skill: Parallelograms and their Properties
feedback:
In parallelograms, the opposite angles are always equal which means they are congruent.
Only consecutive angles in a parallelogram are supplementary, not the opposite angles.
Remember that a parallelogram only has interior angles.

subject: Mathematics
q: How many triangles can be formed by splitting an octagon?
skill: Calculate sum and measure of interior and exterior angles of a polygon
feedback:
An octagon has \[8\] sides. Triangles formed by splitting a polygon are always \[2\] less than the number of sides of the polygon.



Therefore, an octagon can be split into \[6\] triangles.

subject: Mathematics
q: A pentagon can be split into \[3\] triangles. What is the sum of its interior angles?
skill: Calculate sum and measure of interior and exterior angles of a polygon
feedback:
A pentagon can be divided into \[3\] triangles.

\begin{align} \mbox{Sum of interior angles of a triangle }& = 180^\circ\\ \mbox{Number of triangles in a pentagon }& = 3\\ \mbox{Sum of interior angles of a pentagon }& = 3 \times 180\\ & = 540^\circ \end{align}

Therefore, the sum of all interior angles of a pentagon is \[540^\circ\].

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of all interior angles of a triangle is _____.
skill: Calculate sum and measure of interior and exterior angles of a polygon
feedback:
The sum of all interior angles of a triangle is \[180^\circ\].
The sum of all interior angles of a quadrilateral is \[360^\circ\].
\[540^\circ\] is the sum of interior angles of a pentagon, while the sum of interior angles of an octagon is \[720^\circ\].

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, the red line segment is a _____.
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback:
A chord is a straight line segment that joins any two points on the circle and a chord that goes through the centre of the circle is called a diameter.

Therefore, in the given figure, the red line segment is the diameter.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, the red line segment is a _____.
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback: A chord is a straight line segment that joins any two points on the circle. Thus, in the given figure, the red line segment is a chord.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, the line segment \[\overline{CD}\] is a/an _____.
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback:
A chord is a straight line segment that joins any two points on the circle and a chord that goes through the centre of the circle is called a diameter.

Thus, in the given figure, \[\overline{AB}\] is a chord, whereas \[\overline{CD}\] is a diameter.

\[\overline{OA}\], \[\overline{OC}\], and \[\overline{OD}\] are radial segments of the circle.

\[\widehat{AB}\] is a minor arc, whereas \[\widehat{ACB}\] is a major arc.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the figure below, which of the following is a radial segment?
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback:
\[\overline{OA}\], \[\overline{OC}\], and \[\overline{OD}\] are all radial segments of the circle.

\[\overline{AB}\] is a chord, whereas \[\overline{CD}\] is a diameter.

\[\widehat{AB}\] is a minor arc, whereas \[\widehat{ACB}\] is a major arc.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the following figure, what is the curve highlighted in blue called?
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback:
The circumference is the distance around the circle. In other words, the length of the outer boundary of the circle is its circumference.

A part of the circumference is called an arc. The arc could be of any length.

The blue arc is smaller than the red arc and is called the minor arc, whereas the red arc, or bigger arc, is called the major arc.

Thus, the curve, highlighted in blue, is called a minor arc.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the length of the line segment \[\overline{OC}\] is equal to \[8\] cm, then what is the length of the line segment \[\overline{BD}\]?
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback:
The line segment \[\overline{OC}\] is a radial segment and \[\overline{BD}\] is the diameter. The length of a diameter is equal to twice the radius. Thus,

\begin{align} d&=2r \\ &= 2 \times 8 \\ &= 16 \mbox{ cm} \end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: If the length of the line segment \[\overline{OP}\] is equal to \[5\] cm, then what is the length of the line segment \[\overline{OQ}\]?
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback: The line segments \[\overline{OP}\] and \[\overline{OQ}\] are the radial segments and all radial segments of a circle are equal in length. Thus, \[m\overline{OP} = m\overline{OQ} = 5\] cm.

subject: Mathematics
q: Which of the following is a part of a circle?
skill: Describe a circle and its components
feedback: The centre, radius, chord, and diameter are not parts of a circle, but an arc is a part of a circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, what is the region highlighted in red called?
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback: The region highlighted in red is called a segment. By definition, a region bounded by a chord and an arc is a segment.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, what is the curve highlighted in black called?
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback: The curved line around a part of a circle is called an arc. It is any part of the boundary of the circle. If an arc is extended to draw the full circle, it becomes the circumference of the circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, the part highlighted in red is called a _____.
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback: By definition, a region in a circle bounded by \[2\] radii and an arc is called a sector. Therefore, the highlighted part in the given figure is a sector. This can be understood from the following figure:

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, where does point C lie?
options: in the exterior of the circle
in the interior of the circle
in the centre of the circle
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback:
Point C is lying right on the boundary of the circle, therefore, it is neither an interior point nor an exterior point.
Note that points lying on the circumference of a circle are said to be lying ON the circle. Hence, point C is lying on the circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, point A is lying in the _____ of the circle.
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback:
Any points lying outside the circle are said to be in the exterior of the circle. In this figure, point A is seen outside the circle, so it is lying in the exterior of the circle.

Point B is in the interior of the circle, while point C is said to be on the circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: In the given figure, point B is lying in the _____ of the circle.
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback:
In the given circle, we can see that point B is lying inside the circle. Any points inside the circle are said to be lying in the interior of the circle.

Note that point B is not in the centre of the circle.

subject: Mathematics
q: All the points of a circle _____.
options: are at the same distance from the centre
are arranged parallel to its radius
form a line called the diameter
are at different distances from the centre
skill: Concepts related to circles
feedback: All the points of a circle are at the same distance from its centre. This distance from the centre is called the radius.

subject: Mathematics
q:
The figures below show the steps for constructing an equilateral triangle \[ABC\] with a base of \[6\] cm. Arrange these steps in the correct order.

Figure \[1\]:


Figure \[2\]:


Figure \[3\]:


Figure \[4\]:
skill: Construct an equilateral triangle when the base is given
feedback:
The steps for constructing an equilateral triangle \[ABC\] with a base of \[6\] cm are listed below.

Step \[1\]: Draw a line segment \[\overline{AB}\] of \[6\] cm.



Step \[2\]: With the centre at point \[A\], draw an arc of radius \[6\] cm above the line segment \[\overline{AB}\].



Step \[3\]: Similarly, with the centre at point \[B\], draw an arc of the same radius, such that it intersects the previous arc at point \[C\].



Step \[4\]: Lastly, join point \[C\] with points \[A\] and \[B\].



Thus, \[ABC\] is the resulting equilateral triangle.

subject: Mathematics
q: Each angle in an equilateral triangle is equal to _____.
skill: Construct an equilateral triangle when the base is given
feedback:
An equilateral triangle is a triangle in which all three sides are equal in length. All of the three interior angles are congruent.

Since the sum of the three angles of a triangle is equal to \[180^{\circ}\], this means that each angle in an equilateral triangle is equal to \[60^{\circ}\].

\[\frac{180^{\circ}}{3}=60^{\circ}\]

subject: Mathematics
q: A triangle that has \[3\] equal sides and \[3\] equal angles is known as a/an _____ triangle.
skill: Construct an equilateral triangle when the base is given
feedback:
A triangle that has \[3\] equal sides and \[3\] equal angles is known as an equilateral triangle.



A scalene triangle has all \[3\] sides of different length.



An isosceles triangle is a triangle that has two sides of equal length. The two angles opposite the equal sides are also equal.



A right-angled triangle is a triangle in which one angle is a right angle, that is, a \[90^{\circ}\] angle.

subject: Mathematics
q: The following line graph shows a store's earnings for each month. What is the percentage increase in the store's earnings between month 3 and month 4?
skill: Interpret and draw line graphs
feedback:
By looking at the \[x\] and \[y\] coordinates, we can see that the store earned Rs. \[190{,}000\] in month 4 and Rs. \[120{,}000\] in month 3. To calculate the increase in percentage,

\begin{align} \mbox{Percentage increase} &= \frac{\mbox{Increase in amount}}{\mbox{Original amount}} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{\mbox{Month 4 - Month 3}}{\mbox{Month 3}} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{190{,}000 - 120{,}000}{120{,}000} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{70{,}000}{120{,}000} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{7}{12} \times 100\\\\ &=58.33\% \end{align}

The percentage increase in money earned between month 3 and month 4 is \[58.33\%\].
