We can see that the line slopes downwards in two places. The first is in week 2 and the other is in week 4. Looking closely, we can see that the number of movies watched in week 2 is \[5\], whereas the number of movies watched in week 4 is \[4\]. Therefore, Sara watched the least number of movies in week 4.

q: The following graph shows the number of movies Sara watched every week. What is the percentage decrease in movies watched between week 5 and week 6?
skill: Interpret and draw line graphs
feedback:
By looking at the \[x\] and \[y\] coordinates, we can see that she watched \[14\] movies in week 5 and \[9\] movies in week 6. To calculate the decrease in percentage,

\begin{align} \mbox{Percentage decrease} &= \frac{\mbox{Decrease in amount}}{\mbox{Original amount}} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{\mbox{Week 5 - Week 6}}{\mbox{Week 5}} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{14-9}{14} \times 100\\\\ &=\frac{5}{14} \times 100\\\\ &=35.71\% \end{align}

The percentage decrease in movies watched from week 5 to week 6 is \[35.71\%\].

\begin{align} \mbox{Jan to Feb}&= 12 - 9\\ &=3^\circ\mbox{C}\\\\ \mbox{March to April} &= 16 - 10\\ &= 6^\circ\mbox{C}\\\\ \mbox{April to May}&=23 - 16\\ &=7^\circ\mbox{C} \end{align}

We can see that the temperature increased the most between April and May.

q: Use the pie graph given to identify the angle sector that represents people who speak French.
skill: Read a pie graph
feedback:
The central angle of a pie graph is \[360^\circ\] and so, all the angle sectors in the pie graph must add up to \[360^\circ\]. Let the angle for the sector representing French be equal to \[x\].

\[154^\circ + 54^\circ + 72^\circ + x^\circ = 360^\circ\]

\[280^\circ + x = 360^\circ\]

\[x = 360^\circ - 280^\circ\]

\[x = 80^\circ\]

subject: Mathematics
q: The median of four numbers is \[6.5\]. If three of the numbers are \[3, 5,\] and \[9\], then the fourth number is ____.
skill: Describe measures of central tendency and calculate mean, median and mode for ungrouped data
feedback:
If we select \[3\], then the ascending order of data is \[3, 3, 5, 9\] and the median will be \[4\], which is not possible as median is given as \[6.5\].

Similarly if we select \[4\], then the ascending order of data is \[3, 4, 5, 9\] and the median will be \[4.5\], which is also not correct.

Same for \[10\]. If we select \[10\], then the ascending order of the data is \[3, 5, 9, 10\] and the median will be \[7\], which is not correct.

Now for \[8\], the ascending order is \[3, 5, 8, 9\] and the median is \[6.5\] which is correct.

subject: Mathematics
q: Ahmed arranged the number of books owned by his friends in ascending order as \[10, 12, 15, x, 17,\] and \[18\]. If the median of the data is \[16\], then the value of \[x\] is _____.
skill: Describe measures of central tendency and calculate mean, median and mode for ungrouped data
feedback:
\[10, 12, 15, x, 17, 18\]

\[n = 6\]

Since \[n\] is even, median is the arithmetic mean of two middle values. Thus,

\begin{align} \mbox{Median} &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{n}{2} \mbox{th value} + \frac{n+2}{2} \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ 16 &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{6}{2} \mbox{th value} + \frac{6+2}{2} \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ 16 &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{3 \mbox{rd value} + 4 \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ 16 &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{15+ x \right\} \\\\ 16 \times 2 &= 15 + x\\\\ 32 &= 15 + x\\\\ x &= 32-15 \\\\ x &= 17 \end{align}

q: The median of the data \[18, 3, 17, 1, 18, 5,\] and \[2\] is _____.
skill: Describe measures of central tendency and calculate mean, median and mode for ungrouped data
feedback:
First, arrange the data in ascending order.

\[1, 2, 3, 5, 17, 18, 18\]

\[n = 7\]

Since \[n\] is odd, median \[= \frac{n+1}{2}\]th value of data. Thus,

\begin{align} \mbox{Median} &= \frac{n+1}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= \frac{7+1}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= \frac{8}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= 4 \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= 5 \end{align}

q:
The following data represents the number of students absent in a class for \[8\] consecutive days.
\[6, 6, 8, 5, 6, 7, 8, 7\]
The median of the number of students absent is _____.

feedback:
First, arrange the data in ascending order.

\[5, 6, 6, 6, 7, 7, 8, 8\]

\[n = 8\]

Since \[n\] is even, median is the arithmetic mean of two middle values. Thus,

\begin{align} \mbox{Median} &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{n}{2} \mbox{th value} + \frac{n+2}{2} \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{8}{2} \mbox{th value} + \frac{8+2}{2} \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{4 \mbox{th value} + 5 \mbox{th value} \right\} \\\\ &= \frac{1}{2}\left\{6+ 7 \right\} \\\\ &= \frac{13}{2}\\\\ &= 6.5 \end{align}

\[n = 9\]

Since \[n\] is odd, median \[= \frac{n+1}{2}\]th value of data. Thus,

\begin{align} \mbox{Median} &= \frac{n+1}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= \frac{9+1}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= \frac{10}{2} \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= 5 \mbox{th value} \\\\ &= 8 \end{align}

q: The mean width of \[12\] iPads is \[5.1\] inches. What is the total width of \[12\] iPads?

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of ten numbers is \[58\]. If one of the numbers is \[40\], what is the mean of the other nine?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback: \begin{align}\mbox{Mean} &= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all numbers}}{\mbox{Total number of observations}}\\\\58&= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all numbers}}{10}\\\\580&= \mbox{Sum of all numbers}\\\\580 -40 &= \mbox{Sum of nine numbers}\\540&=\mbox{Sum of nine numbers}\\\\\mbox{Mean of nine numbers} & = \frac{540}{9}\\\\\mbox{Mean of nine numbers} & = 60\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of four numbers is \[71.5\]. If three of the numbers are \[58, 76,\] and \[88\], what is the value of the fourth number?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback: \begin{align}\mbox{Let the fourth number be} &=x\\\mbox{Mean} &= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all observations }}{\mbox{Total number of observations}}\\\\71.5&= \frac{58+76+88+x}{4}\\\\71.5 \times 4&= 222+x\\286 &= 222+x\\x&= 286-222\\x&=64\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q: The mean of \[9\] observations is \[16\]. One more observation is included and the new mean becomes \[17\]. What is the \[10^{\text{th}}\] observation?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback: \begin{align}\mbox{Mean} &= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all observations }}{\mbox{Total number of observations}}\\\\16&= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all observations }}{9}\\\\144 &= \mbox{Sum of all observations}\\\\\mbox{After including one more observation}, & \mbox{ we get}\\\\17&= \frac{\mbox{Sum of all observations }}{10}\\\\170 &= \mbox{Sum of all observations}\\\\10^{\text{th}}\mbox{ observation} &=170-144\\10^{\text{th}}\mbox{ observation}&=26\end{align}

subject: Mathematics

skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)

subject: Mathematics
q: For a set of \[n\] observations \[x_1,x_2,…,x_n\], we can find its arithmetic mean by using an assumed mean \[A\]. It is given that \[∑D=50\], where \[D=x-A\], and the arithmetic mean is \[20\]. Which of the following relationships between \[A\] and \[n\] is true?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
To find the arithmetic mean using assumed mean, we use the following formula:

\[\bar{X}=A+\frac{∑D}{n}\].

In this case, we have

\[20=A+\frac{50}{n}\]

\[A=20-\frac{50}{n}\].

Note: Remember that we first obtain the deviation of each observation from the assumed mean \[(D=x-A)\] and then compute the average deviation. After that, we add back the assumed mean.

subject: Mathematics
q: Given that the arithmetic mean of \[88, 70, 172, 125, 115, 95, x, 81, 90, 95, 90\] is \[\sqrt{x}+95 \], find the value of \[x\] correct to \[1\] decimal place.
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
The arithmetic mean is given by

\[\bar{X}=\frac{∑x}{n}=\frac{88+70+172+125+115+95+x+81+90+95+90}{11}=\sqrt{x}+95\]

Simplifying:

\[\frac{1021+x}{11}=\sqrt{x}+95\]

\[1021+x=11\sqrt{x}+1045 \]

\[x-24=11\sqrt{x} \]

\[(x-24)^2=121x \]

\[576-48x+x^2=121x \]

\[x^2-169x+576=0\]

Applying the quadratic formula:

\[x=\frac{\left ( 169±\sqrt{169^2-4(576)} \right )}{2}\]

\[x=\frac{\left ( 169±\sqrt{28561-2304} \right )}{2}\]

\[x=\frac{\left ( 169±\sqrt{26257} \right )}{2}\]

\[x=\frac{\left ( 169± 162.04 \right )}{2}\]

\[x=\frac{331.04}{2}\;\;\; \mbox{or}\;\;\; x= \frac{6.96}{2}\]

\[x ≈ 165.5\;\;\;\;\;\; \mbox{or}\;\;\; x ≈ 3.48\]

Since we have squared both sides in solving the radical equation, we have to check for extraneous roots.
\[x≈3.48\] is an extraneous root here.
Therefore, \[x= 165.5\].

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of \[n\] observations, \[∑_nx_i\] is \[120\] and the arithmetic mean is \[n+7\]. What is the value of \[n\]?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
Applying the formula \[\bar{X}=\frac{∑x}{n}\], we get

\[\frac{120}{n}=n+7\]

\[120=n^2+7n \]

This is a quadratic equation, which can be solved by applying the quadratic formula:

\[n^2+7n-120=0 \]

\[n=\frac{-7±\sqrt{49-4(-120)}}{2}\]

\[n=\frac{-7±\sqrt{49+480}}{2}\]

\[n=\frac{-7±\sqrt{529}}{2}\]

\[n=\frac{-7± 23}{2}\]

\[n=\frac{-7 + 23}{2}\;\;\; \mbox{or} \;\;\; n=\frac{-7 - 23}{2} \]

\[n=\frac{16}{2}\;\;\; \mbox{or}\;\;\;n=\frac{-30}{2} \]

\[n=8\;\;\;\;\;\; \mbox{or}\;\;\;n=-15 \]

Since \[n\] is the number of observations, it must be positive. Therefore, \[n=8\].

subject: Mathematics
q: A set of data contains the following numbers \[70\], \[100\], \[150\], \[130\], \[140\], \[150\],\[x\], \[60\], \[110\], \[600\]. It is given that the arithmetic mean of the data is \[160\]. What is the value of \[x\]?
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
For this group of data, the arithmetic mean can be calculated in the following way:

\begin{align}\bar{X}&=\frac{∑x}{n} \\\\\bar{X}&=\frac{70+100+150+130+140+150+x+60+110+600}{10}\\\\160&=\frac{70+100+150+130+140+150+x+60+110+600}{10}\\\\160&=\frac{1510+x}{10}\\\\1600&= 1510+x\\x&=1600-1510\\x&=90\end{align}

subject: Mathematics
q:
The lengths of \[20\] items, \[x_1,x_2,x_3,…,x_{20}\] (in cm) are summarised below.
Assumed mean, \[A = 100\] cm and \[∑u_i=2\], where \[u_i=\frac{x_i-A}{h}\] and the unit of \[u_i\] is metre.
Find the arithmetic mean of the length in cm.
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
Since the length of the items, \[x_i\], is in cm and the unit of \[u_i\] is metre, we know that \[h=100\]. Therefore, to find the mean length in cm, we will use the formula, \[\bar{X}=A+\frac{∑u_i}{n}\times h\], where \[A = 100\], \[n= 20\] and \[h= 100\]. Thus,

\[\bar{X}=100+\frac{2}{20}×100\]

\[\bar{X}=100+\frac{200}{20}\]

\[\bar{X}=100+10\]

\[\bar{X}=110\] cm

skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
We can apply the direct method formula:

\[\bar{X}=\frac{∑fx}{∑f} \] where \[x\] is the midpoint of each class.

Therefore, we have the following:

\[\bar{X}=\frac{4.5×2+14.5×10+24.5×5+34.5×9+44.5×6+54.5×7+64.5×1}{2+10+5+9+6+7+1}\]

subject: Mathematics
q:
What is the arithmetic mean for a set of grouped data if \[D=x-25\], \[∑fD=300\], and \[∑f=20\]?
\[f\] denotes the frequency of an observation and \[D\] denotes the deviation of an observation from the provisional mean of \[25\].
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
From the given information, we can apply the indirect method’s formula for grouped data:

\[\bar{X}=A+\frac{∑fD}{∑f}\]

In this case, the assumed mean \[A=25\]. Hence, we get

\[\bar{X}=25+\frac{300}{20}\]

\[\bar{X}=25+15\]

\[\bar{X}=40\]

Important point:Take note that \[\bar{X}≠\frac{∑fD}{∑f}\].

subject: Mathematics
q: If we want to find the arithmetic mean by using an assumed mean \[A=24\] for the following data \[12\], \[14\], \[17\], \[20\], \[24\], \[29\], \[35\], \[45\], which of the following is the correct step?
options: Find the sum of the given numbers
skill: Calculate arithmetic mean by definition and using deviation from assumed mean (grouped and ungrouped)
feedback:
According to our assumption \[A = 24\], we form the following table by calculating \[D=x_i-A\]:
\[X\]
\[D=X-A\]
\[12\]
\[12-24=-12\]
\[14\]
\[14-24=-10\]
\[17\]
\[17-24=-7\]
\[20\]
\[20-24=-4\]
\[24\]
\[24-24=0\]
\[29\]
\[29-24=5\]
\[35\]
\[35-24=11\]
\[45\]
\[45-24=21\]
\[n=8\]
\[\sum D=4\]
Recall and apply the relevant formula:

\[\bar{X}=A+\frac{∑D}{n}\]

\[=24+\frac{4}{8}\]

\[=24+\frac{1}{2}\]

\[=24.5\]

So the correct step would be to calculate \[x_i-A\] to get \[-12\], \[-10\], \[-7\], \[-4\], \[0\], \[5\], \[11\], \[21\].

skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback: When there are many values in a data set, we group the data into classes of equal class intervals, and then calculate their frequencies.

q: The difference between the lower limits of two consecutive classes is equal to the
skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback:
When we represent the frequency distribution of a large data set, we group it into classes, where the size of each class is called its class interval. The size of these class intervals is always the same. The difference between the lower class limits of two consecutive classes is the class interval.

subject: Mathematics
q: In a frequency distribution table, the sum of all frequencies must be equal to the
options: sum of all the values in a data set.
number of classes in a data set.
number of class intervals in a data set.
number of values in a data set.
skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback: Frequency represents the number of times each value occurs in a data set. Therefore, the sum of the frequency of all the values is always equal to the total number of values in the data set.

subject: Mathematics
q: The ____________ is a way of constructing a frequency distribution table.
skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback: There are two ways of constructing a frequency distribution table: the tally method and direct observation. When using the tally mark method, we keep count of observations using tally bars.

subject: Mathematics
q: A frequency distribution is the arrangement of data in the form of a table that consists of
options: class intervals and class boundaries only.
the lowest and highest value in a data set.
the frequency of each value or group in a data set.
skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback: Frequency distribution is the arrangement of data in the form of a table that shows the frequency of each value for ungrouped data, and the frequency of each group of values for grouped data.

subject: Mathematics
q: The frequency of a value is the
options: number of times it occurs in a class.
total number of values in a data set.
number of times it occurs in a data set.
total number of values in a class.
skill: Define and construct frequency distributions
feedback: The frequency of a value is the number of times it occurs in a data set. For example, in the data \[5\], \[6\], \[2\], \[5\], \[5\], \[3\], the frequency of the value \[5\] is \[3\], because \[5\]  occurs three times in the data set. The frequency of the values \[2\], \[6\] and \[3\] is \[1\], because they all occur once.
